subcodes.first_line: Mortal charm shall fade away, roses shall give way to thorns, and beauty and youth shall live their day and be no more.
subcodes.first_line_orig: ای مفتون جمال حقیقت حسنها زائل گردد و گلها خار برویاند
subcodes.subjects: @transcendence; unknowability of God, @transience; worthlessness of the physical world
full_text_parts: Mortal charm shall fade away, roses shall give way to thorns, and beauty and youth shall live their day and be no more. But that which eternally endureth is the Beauty of the True One, for its splendor perisheth not and its glory lasteth forever; its charm is all-powerful and its attraction infinite. Well is it then with that countenance that reflecteth the splendor of the Light of the Beloved One! The Lord be praised, thou hast been illumined with this Light, hast acquired the pearl of true knowledge, and hast spoken the Word of Truth.
